figures.caption: Fig. 1. Nucleophilic metabolites scavenge acyl-CoAs to prevent non-enzymatic protein acylation. (B) Extracted ion chromatograms (EICs) of NTSEs. Indicated acyl-CoAs (acetyl-, succinyl-, glutaryl-, HMG-, and butyryl-CoA) were incubated in the pres absence of scavengers (10 mM each of ethanolamine [Ea], taurine [Tau], or spermidine [Spd]) at pH 8.0, 37 °C f (C) Time-course quantification of N1- and N8-acetylspermidine formation from an in vitro reaction of 1 mM ace

Fig. 2. Spermidine prevents p300-mediated acetylation via nucleophilic scavenging (A) Exper workflow for metabolomics analysis of HEK293T cells transfected with the V5-tagged p300 HAT (residues 1048–1664; hereafter p300-HAT-V5). (B) Volcano plot showing the differential metabolome o HAT-V5-overexpressing vs. mock-transfected cells (n = 3). Acetylated polyamines are highlighted. Meta were putatively annotated by molecular formula and ChemSpider database matching. P-values were cal using an unpaired two-tailed Welch's t-test. (C) Chemical structures and intracellular levels of monoacetylated polyamines in p300-HAT-V5-overexpressing cells. (D) MS/MS spectra identifying the in acetylated polyamines shown in (C). (E) Immunoblot analysis of acetyllysine and V5 in p300-HA transfected HEK293T cells treated with or without 1 mM spermidine for 18 h. (F) Quantification of acetylation from (E), normalized to p300 expression (IAcK / IV5). (G) In vitro quantification of enzyma non-enzymatic N8-acetylspermidine production over time. Purified p300 HAT domain (1048–1664 AA, was incubated with 1 mM acetyl-CoA and 5 mM spermidine. (H) Kinetic analysis of in vitro p300 mediated N8-acetylspermidine production with spermidine.

Fig. 4. Taurine supplementation promotes N-fatty acyl taurine formation by intercepting fatty acy in high-fat diet–fed mice. (A) Experimental scheme validating the in vivo scavenging reaction betwee acyl-CoA and taurine. Plasma and fecal samples were analyzed by LC-MS/MS to detect N-fatty acyl (B) Body weight changes in C57BL/6N mice fed a high-fat diet (HFD) with (n=6) or without (n=6) supplementation (5% w/v in drinking water), compared to chow diet controls (n=4). (C, D) Volcano plot (C) plasma and (D) fecal lipidomes comparing taurine-treated vs. mock-treated HFD mice. (E) Fragme pattern and MS/MS spectrum of N-stearoyl taurine. (F) Levels of (left) oleoyl-, stearoyl-, and palmitoyl in feces, and (right) oleoyl- and stearoyl-taurine in plasma collected via submandibular (cheek) bleed. D mean ± SD; n = 3; *P < 0.05, unpaired two-tailed Welch's t-test.
